changesProposed.name: AST SpaceMobile

verified.name: AST SpaceMobile
verified.description: Publicly traded satellite designer and manufacturer building the SpaceMobile constellation, a space-based cellular broadband network designed to connect directly to standard, unmodified smartphones to deliver 4G and 5G coverage globally.
verified.notes: Founded as AST & Science LLC in May 2017; became AST SpaceMobile via SPAC merger in April 2021 and began trading on Nasdaq. Wikipedia lists website as ast-science.com while company website header shows astspacemobile.com; both are verified in sources.

changesProposed.name: Atom Computing

verified.name: Atom Computing
verified.description: Quantum computing company developing gate-based quantum computers using arrays of optically-trapped neutral atoms. The company has announced over 1,000-qubit systems and is collaborating with Microsoft on fault-tolerant logical qubits.
verified.notes: Wikipedia entry mentions Rob Hayes served as CEO in 2021, but company website identifies Ben Bloom as Founder & CEO and Rob Hays as CEO & President with different spelling. The database entry references a 2025 sale of Magne system to QuNorth, but this is not verified in provided sources. Company website shows press release dated May 21, 2026 regarding $100M Letter of Intent with U.S. Department of Commerce, and June 3, 2026 announcements about quantum error correction, indicating sources are from future dates relative to Wikipedia's last update.

verified.description: Geothermal energy company that generates electricity through enhanced geothermal systems (EGS) using horizontal drilling and distributed fiber optic sensing technologies. Operates Project Red demonstration facility in Nevada and is developing Cape Station in Utah.

Series E funding of $462M mentioned in source [2] from November 2025, but total_raised figure of $1.89B from IPO is more recent.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

current.name: Starcloud
current.founder: Philip Johnston, Ezra Feilden, Adi Oltean

verified.name: Starcloud
verified.description: United States-based company that designs, builds, and deploys data centers in space using proprietary technology. Launched Starcloud-1 satellite with Nvidia H100 GPU in November 2025.
verified.notes: Originally founded as Lumen Orbit in January 2024 in El Segundo, California; rebranded to Starcloud in March 2025 following legal challenge from Lumen Technologies. Became fastest unicorn in Y Combinator history at 17 months after completing program (March 2026). Series A funding announced March 30, 2026.
